varieties.name: Sicot 1
varieties.growing_notes: Founded modern Australian cotton breeding; established Sicot series
varieties.availability: Historical significance; no longer commercially available

varieties.name: Siokra 1-1
varieties.traits: Okra leaf shape (narrow leaflets), bacterial blight resistant
varieties.growing_notes: First okra leaf variety grown worldwide; eliminated bacterial blight from Australian industry; water-use efficient
varieties.availability: Historical heirloom status; replaced by modern varieties; okra leaf trait continues in modern breeding

varieties.name: Sicala V-1
varieties.growing_notes: First CSIRO variety with significant Verticillium wilt resistance
varieties.availability: Historical - superseded by newer resistant varieties

varieties.name: Sicala 3-1
varieties.growing_notes: Major variety of 1990s; part of Sicala series development

varieties.name: Sicala 40
varieties.growing_notes: 16% of seed sales in first year (1998); rapid adoption success story

varieties.name: Sicot F-1
varieties.traits: Fusarium wilt resistance (double that of 1994 benchmark)
varieties.growing_notes: Critical for managing alkaline clay soil Fusarium wilt in Australia

varieties.name: Sicot 71BRF
varieties.traits: Bollgard II + Roundup Ready Flex, exceptional fiber quality, dryland adapted
varieties.growing_notes: Added $60M+ value in first year; enabled dryland expansion; strong Texas USA performance as FiberMax; 85% insecticide reduction; subtropical Queensland performance proven

varieties.name: Sicot 74BRF
varieties.traits: Bollgard II + Roundup Ready Flex, improved over Sicot 71
varieties.growing_notes: 3-4% yield increase over Sicot 71; enhanced staple, strength, fineness

varieties.name: Sicala 340BRF
varieties.growing_notes: Replaced Sicala 350B; subtropical adaptation

varieties.name: Siokra 24BRF
varieties.traits: Bollgard II + Roundup Ready Flex, okra leaf, delayed whitefly resistance
varieties.growing_notes: Improved over Siokra V16BR; suitable dryland conditions; subtropical Queensland suitability

varieties.name: Sicot 620
varieties.traits: Cotton Bunchy Top virus resistance, Bt traits
varieties.growing_notes: First CBT-resistant cultivar using marker-assisted selection; critical for Queensland production

varieties.name: Sicot 746B3F
varieties.growing_notes: General-purpose variety for subtropical Queensland; reliable performance across environments
varieties.availability: Cotton Seed Distributors (CSD) - current major variety

varieties.name: Sicot 754B3F
varieties.growing_notes: Specialty variety for quality fiber production; subtropical suitable
varieties.availability: Cotton Seed Distributors (CSD) - current specialty variety

varieties.name: Sicot 714B3F
varieties.growing_notes: Used as recurrent parent for breeding programs; proven subtropical performance

varieties.name: PAU Bt 1
varieties.growing_notes: Developed for northern India but tested in Australia; important as non-hybrid Bt variety

varieties.name: Queensland Cotton (Colonial Era)
varieties.growing_notes: Grown since 1800s but production was small and variable until modern irrigated industry (1960s)
varieties.availability: Historical heritage only; not commercially available

varieties.name: Karunganni
varieties.traits: Short-staple, dark seeds, drought-resistant, pest-tolerant, rainfed adapted
varieties.growing_notes: 5,000+ year heritage; grown with intercropping (coriander, onions, lady finger, millets, pulses); Geographical Indication status; revival by KASKOM and Tula movements; SITRA processing successful
varieties.availability: Sahaja Samrudha, KASKOM, Tula India, select organic farmers Tamil Nadu; limited seed availability

varieties.name: Ponduru Cotton
varieties.traits: Two varieties: Konda patti (hill cotton) and Yerra patti (red/cream cotton); short-staple, drought-resistant
varieties.growing_notes: Patnulu handspun khadi tradition; UNESCO-worthy heritage craft; hand-picked, manually ginned using catfish jawbone; Gandhi charkha spinning; Geographical Handloom Identification tag; celebrated by Mahatma Gandhi
varieties.availability: Ponduru village spinners/weavers, Andhra Pradesh Khadi boards; 300 spinners, 350 weavers active

varieties.name: Jayadhar
varieties.growing_notes: Karnataka indigenous variety; grown with chili and vegetables intercropping; organic cultivation suitable; monsoon adapted
varieties.availability: Organic farmers Karnataka; Sahaja Samrudha; limited commercial availability

varieties.name: Comilla (Khil)
varieties.traits: Grown with 15+ intercropping species including short-duration paddy; adapted to high rainfall
varieties.growing_notes: North-Eastern Hill region specialty; extreme monsoon adaptation (3,000mm+ rainfall); indigenous to high-humidity tropical climate; suitable tropical monsoon climates monsoon conditions
varieties.availability: CICR germplasm collection; North-East organic farmers; very limited commercial availability

varieties.name: Bengal Desi
varieties.traits: Traditional Bengal cotton; historically famous for Dhaka muslin
varieties.growing_notes: Phuti karpas variant used for legendary Dhaka muslin (grown south of Dhaka along Meghna River); historical cultural significance; high tensile strength at fine counts; tropical monsoon adapted
varieties.availability: Very limited - conservation collections only; historical importance

varieties.name: Mathio (Mathieu)
varieties.growing_notes: Central India traditional variety; collected by CICR; monsoon adapted
varieties.availability: CICR germplasm collection; limited commercial availability

varieties.name: Waghad
varieties.growing_notes: Gujarat traditional variety; extremely hardy; suitable for marginal lands; organic cultivation
varieties.availability: Organic farmers Gujarat; limited commercial availability

varieties.name: Konda Pathi
varieties.traits: Hill cotton; drought and pest resistant
varieties.growing_notes: Andhra Pradesh hill variety; monsoon adapted; used for Ponduru textiles
varieties.availability: Local Andhra farmers; limited commercial seed

varieties.name: Yerra Pathi
varieties.growing_notes: Andhra Pradesh traditional; distinctive cream-colored lint; monsoon suitable
varieties.availability: Local Andhra farmers; limited commercial seed

varieties.name: MCU-5
varieties.growing_notes: Historical Tamil Nadu variety; Coimbatore development; 2,000-4,000 ha cultivation supported by SIMA; monsoon adapted
varieties.availability: South India Mills Association (SIMA) seed distribution; Tamil Nadu Agricultural University

varieties.name: MCU-7
varieties.growing_notes: Currently cultivated in Tamil Nadu; reliable performance; monsoon suitable
varieties.availability: Tamil Nadu seed agencies; agricultural universities

varieties.name: MCU 5 VT
varieties.growing_notes: Disease-resistant variety for South India; tropical performance; important for disease-prone areas

varieties.name: Suvin
varieties.traits: Extra-long staple (ELS), premium quality, intensive management
varieties.growing_notes: King of Indian cotton; finest and longest staple cotton globally; vulnerable to climate change and sucking pests; requires assured irrigation; declining production due to erratic rainfall; heritage premium variety
varieties.availability: CICR Coimbatore maintains pure seed stock; very limited commercial cultivation

varieties.name: LRA 5166
varieties.growing_notes: Currently cultivated Tamil Nadu; good fiber quality; monsoon performance

varieties.name: SVPR 2 (Surabhi)
varieties.growing_notes: Modern Tamil Nadu variety; current commercial cultivation; tropical monsoon suitable

varieties.name: K10
varieties.growing_notes: Modern desi variety for rainfed conditions; Tamil Nadu cultivation; monsoon adapted

varieties.name: K11
varieties.growing_notes: Modern desi variety; rainfed suitable; tropical conditions

varieties.name: AKH 4
varieties.growing_notes: Maharashtra development; rainfed cultivation; adaptable to monsoon variability

varieties.name: AKH 5
varieties.growing_notes: Maharashtra variety; heat and drought adaptation; suitable for tropical regions

varieties.name: DHY 286
varieties.growing_notes: Pest-resistant variety; Maharashtra development; monsoon suitable; currently cultivated

varieties.name: Purnima
varieties.growing_notes: Modern Maharashtra variety; reliable monsoon performance

varieties.name: Rajat
varieties.growing_notes: Maharashtra cultivation; tropical monsoon adapted

varieties.name: PKV 081
varieties.growing_notes: Maharashtra development; pest resistance; suitable for monsoon variability; approved for release

varieties.name: G.Cot 12
varieties.growing_notes: Gujarat variety; heat and drought tolerance; monsoon suitable

varieties.name: G.Cot 13
varieties.growing_notes: Gujarat herbaceum variety; extremely hardy; suitable for marginal conditions

varieties.name: G.Cot 15 (Sanjay)
varieties.growing_notes: Gujarat arboreum variety; organic-suitable; rainfed adapted

varieties.name: G.Cot 16
varieties.growing_notes: Modern Gujarat variety; reliable performance

varieties.name: G.Cot 17
varieties.growing_notes: Hardy herbaceum variety; marginal land suitable

varieties.name: G.Cot 19
varieties.growing_notes: Modern desi variety; sustainable agriculture focus

varieties.name: H-4
varieties.traits: World's first cotton hybrid; Dr. C.T. Patel development
varieties.growing_notes: Historic significance: world's first commercial cotton hybrid; established hybrid breeding globally; father of hybrid cotton (Dr. C.T. Patel)
varieties.availability: Historical significance only; modern hybrids have replaced

varieties.name: Bikaneri Nerma
varieties.growing_notes: Indian-bred Bt variety (not hybrid); important for domestic breeding

varieties.name: NHH-44
varieties.growing_notes: Indian-bred hybrid with pest resistance; widely grown

varieties.name: Acala 1517-88
varieties.traits: Acala type, San Joaquin Valley adapted, medium-long staple
varieties.growing_notes: National standard variety USA (1993-1995); excellent fiber quality; influenced Australian breeding
varieties.availability: Historical - USA varieties not typically grown in Australia/India; genetic parent influence

varieties.name: Deltapine 50
varieties.traits: Acala type for San Joaquin Valley, short maturity, good fiber quality
varieties.growing_notes: National standard USA variety; short maturity reduced costs; historical importance in USA cotton breeding

varieties.name: Deltapine 90
varieties.traits: High yield Acala, premium fiber, wide adaptation
varieties.growing_notes: National standard USA (1993-1995); widely adapted; influenced global cotton breeding including Australia

varieties.name: Stoneville 2B
varieties.growing_notes: Founded Stoneville brand (100-year legacy); early commercial success; historical breeding importance

varieties.name: Paymaster HS-26
varieties.growing_notes: National standard USA variety; stress tolerance traits important for breeding programs
